title: Install man pages from containers image as well as skopeo

comments.body: Closes: https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/225
@mtrmac @aweiteka @vrothberg PTAL
Tests aren't hip, complaining of a dirty tree.
Changes LGTM if you can figure out the git unhappiness.

Please don't merge the PR. The problem is that this only works with `vndr` but we're about to move to `go mod` where whitelisting paths is not possible. In fact, I opened a PR yesterday https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/644.
@vrothberg Have to think how we can fix this as we go forward.  Ways to install these files?

> @vrothberg Have to think how we can fix this as we go forward. Ways to install these files?

For the packages, we could make them multi-source packages.
@vrothberg Yes although we then need to make sure the spec file pulls the matching containers/image with the skopeo package.

Also @lsm5 Is not a big fan of doing this.
Containers-common,  should probably become its own package and grab the latest released version of containers/storage and containers/image man pages and config files.

> @vrothberg Have to think how we can fix this as we go forward. Ways to install these files?

Well, there is still a `Makefile`. We could add a `make dist` that downloads the _correct_ corresponding version of c/image, extracts the image, and builds a tarball that we can use as a source in the RPM.

Doing this in the spec file would be difficult because spec files don’t have network access.  Of course the RPM could have two source items, but ensuring c/image matches the vendored version would be manual work; or externally-scripted work that could be overlooked.

Up to @lsm5 really.

@vrothberg @mtrmac I reworked this pr to pull directly from github the man pages and install them
PTAL

@rhatdan @vrothberg so in the ubuntu package, I install `registries.conf` from containers/image vendored inside skopeo (need to do this for the rpms as well). I believe that file has been whitelisted somewhere. Can we do the same thing for the docs? So, as long as the right version is vendored, we can still keep it a single source rpm.
@lsm5 we will soon use `go mod` instead of `vndr` for dependency management. `go mod` does not support whitelisting, so we need to find an alternative approach.

> To be on the very safe side, we could explicitly exclude `github.com/containers/image/image.docs` in the `go.mod` file to prevent confusing go modules.

I hope the tool does not allow mixing&matching different subpackages of c/image (as long as that subpackage is not explicitly a separate Go module) (or does it?), so this seems like something that should not be necessary.

I actually do not think this will work in Fedora builds.  I believe Brew is cut off from the internet, to make sure nothing gets pulled down during the build that does not get put in the lookaside cache.  @lsm5 Could you confirm?
Same applies to openSUSE as well. It would require to ship the source tarballs.
Then I think we need to make this optional?  Or the packagers do not do make install-docs.

> I actually do not think this will work in Fedora builds. I believe Brew is cut off from the internet, to make sure nothing gets pulled down during the build that does not get put in the lookaside cache. 

Yes.

> Then I think we need to make this optional? Or the packagers do not do make install-docs.

That’s not quite the issue; omitting `make install-docs` would remove the internet dependency, but it would also remove the man pages from the package.

_Installing_ the docs is not something the RPM packaging strictly needs help with, we can always add extra shell code into the spec file.

We need a way to _deliver_ the man pages into the RPM.  That could be a `make dist` in this repo that creates a tarball, it could be a separate update-Fedora-RPM script that downloads the man pages and commits them into the Fedora package repo directly, it could be something else.

Neither `make image.docs` nor `make install` is sufficient to handle the delivery process.

How about we just create a separate `containers-image` package, get the docs installed in there, and maybe have containers-common require it? Shouldn't take that long to get done, and we need not have more than 1 source tarball per rpm, I really don't wanna go our prior docker rpm route :D . Jindrich and I can get that done.
If you are going to do this, you need to also do it with container-storage, which also has man pages.

> If you are going to do this, you need to also do it with container-storage, which also has man pages.

I'm cool with that.
Moving this effort to https://github.com/containers/image/pull/661
